mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive when policy stays near the effective lower bound and do not accelerate upward. The signal fails if rate cuts occur alongside recessionary deterioration that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS is below 1% or trending lower, or if triggered periods produce negative average returns in a majority of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates firm nominal consumer demand and revenue momentum for large-cap firms, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next month. The effect fails when sales strength is already fully priced or when it is followed by a simultaneous demand shock.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 1.5%
failure_condition: Rejected if RSAFS breakouts above the 6-month average do not coincide with positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE signals improving labor market slack absorption and stronger household income prospects, which supports equities with a lag as earnings expectations rise. The signal is falsified if unemployment declines during a profit recession and stocks still underperform over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after 3-month UNRATE declines, or if the signal shows no improvement versus unconditional returns in any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates and PAYEMS expands together, firms are increasing output while adding jobs, a combination that indicates broad cyclical improvement and should raise S&P 500 forward returns. The effect fails when production rises without employment growth, implying inventory or productivity-driven gains rather than demand strength.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint positive changes in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series triggers, or if conditional 20-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration combined with a FEDFUNDS rate that is still materially below inflation signals a policy lag and rising real-rate pressure, which should depress equity valuations over the next month. The mechanism fails when inflation rises but policy tightens quickly enough to restore real-rate stability.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS remains below CPI inflation by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Rejected if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not generate negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive after controlling for the same inflation regime over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST above trend signals housing demand stabilization and wealth-channel support for consumption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when housing starts improve only because of temporary volatility and not because broader demand is strengthening.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Rejected if housing-start rebounds fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return after the trigger is not greater than the unconditional 60-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates weakening real activity and shrinking labor demand, which should lead S&P 500 returns to be negative over the next month as earnings revisions turn down. The signal fails if employment weakness is isolated but production remains strong enough to offset recession fears.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Rejected if two-month joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS are not followed by negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not below 45% positive over the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is falling and PAYEMS is rising at the same time, labor-market momentum improves household income expectations and reduces recession odds, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll gains are concentrated in low-quality rebounds that do not translate into broader spending.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Rejected if concurrent unemployment declines and payroll gains do not deliver positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional return distribution is indistinguishable from baseline across rolling 2-year periods.
